title: Fort Mason Historic District Walking Tour - Fort Mason Center for Arts & Culture
content: ## July 25 @ 2:00 pm – 3:30 pm. ## Fort Mason Historic District Walking Tour. San Francisco City Guides, in partnership with Fort Mason Center for Arts & Culture, presents a free tour of Fort Mason, a National Historic Landmark District. Meet at the Gatehouse (Equator Coffees Café) for an invigorating stroll through the Fort Mason Center for Arts & Culture campus and adjacent historical areas. Explore the evolution of this unique historical landmark from its dynamic origins as a military post and the San Francisco Port of Embarkation for troops shipping out during World War II to its current role as a vibrant contemporary cultural venue. View the Lobos Square and park area and learn more about how this space became a refuge for survivors of the 1906 earthquake and fire. The Gatehouse (Equator Coffees Café), Fort Mason Center for Arts & Culture, 2 Marina Boulevard, San Francisco. Sign up today for the latest news from Fort Mason Center For Arts & Culture.

title: Fort Mason Historic District (U.S. National Park Service)
content: An official website of the United States government. **.gov** website belongs to an official government. Although Fort Mason was originally established as a coastal fortification in the 1860s, it is best remembered as headquarters of the San Francisco Port of Embarkation between 1910 and 1963. It is exporting war." "From the early days of the campaigns in the Southwest Pacific, when men and supplies available to reinforce our position were but a trickle, to the time when with added resources we were enabled to mount offensive operations with increasing violence," wrote General Douglas MacArthur, the U.S. Army's San Francisco Port of Embarkation and its subsidiary Oakland Army Terminal, "gave magnificently of their full support--support which in no small measure contributed to the victorious march which carried our arms to the heart of the Japanese Empire.". It is this section of Fort Mason , known as San Francisco Port of Embarkation, U.S. Army, that has been designated a National Historic Landmark. Fort Mason is located between Fisherman's Wharf and the Golden Gate Bridge on San Francisco Bay, in San Francisco's Marina district.
